theory.intro: A line graph plots points and joins them, usually to show how something changes over time. You read values by following the scales on the two axes.
facts: The **horizontal axis** usually shows time; the **vertical axis** shows the measured value.
Each gridline step is worth a fixed amount — read the scale before reading any point.
A line going **up** means the value is increasing; going **down** means decreasing; flat means no change.
steps: Read the scale on each axis to find what one interval is worth.
Go up from the time on the horizontal axis to the line.
Read straight across to the vertical axis for the value.

foundation.q: The graph shows the temperature each hour. What was the temperature at $2$ pm?
Using the same temperature graph, what was the temperature at $4$ pm?
The graph shows a plant's height each week. How tall was it in week $3$?
Using the plant graph, in which week was the plant $25\text{ cm}$ tall?
The graph shows rainfall each day. How much rain fell on day $1$?
Using the rainfall graph, how much rain fell on day $4$?
The graph shows a child's mass each year. What was the mass in year $2$?
The graph shows visitors each month. How many visitors in month $5$?
The graph shows the height of a candle each hour. What height at hour $3$?
The graph shows distance travelled each hour. What distance at hour $4$?

development.q: The graph shows temperature each hour. Between which two hours did the temperature rise the most?
Using the same graph, what was the temperature at hour $1$?
The graph shows sales each day. On how many days were sales above $\$30$?
The graph shows attendance each week. What was the increase from week $1$ to week $3$?
The graph shows water depth each hour. Between which hours did the depth stay the same?
The graph shows temperature. By how much did it fall from hour $4$ to hour $5$?
The graph shows a journey's distance. How far had the cyclist gone after $3$ hours?
The graph shows a pool filling. For how many hours did it stay at $40\text{ L}$?
The graph shows two readings, $A$ and $B$, each hour. Which line is higher at hour $4$?
The graph shows temperature each hour. What was the highest temperature reached?

mastery.q: The graph shows temperature each hour. The reading at hour $3$ is missing. If it sits halfway between hours $2$ and $4$, what is it?
The graph shows distance over time. Over the $5$ hours, what was the total distance travelled?
The graph shows temperature. For how many hours was the temperature above $20^{\circ}\text{C}$?
The graph shows two cyclists' distances. At which hour did they meet (same distance)?
A line graph rises steadily from $\$0$ to $\$50$ over $5$ weeks of saving. How much was saved per week?
The graph shows temperature. What was the difference between the highest and lowest readings?
The graph shows a balloon's height. After rising, when did it begin to descend?
Sales rose from $\$20$ in week $1$ to $\$50$ in week $4$. What was the average rise per week?
A line graph of temperature is flat at $40^{\circ}\text{C}$ for hours $2$ to $5$. What does the flat line tell you?
The graph shows temperature each hour. Estimate the temperature at $2.5$ hours, between the two plotted points.
